threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the 30-minute wait for a bus is outrageous. We're a '15 minutes away' type of people, even when it takes an hour.
Caught a night bus last week, driver was blasting reggae. Everyone was vibing until a rat darted in, and we all froze mid-sway.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw kids playing football in the street while their parents chatted over coffee at the corner kiosk. Pure Neukölln chaos.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you see DJs spinning records while it snows, creating a surreal winter rave vibe on the East Side Gallery.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their reaction to a siren – they hardly flinch, while newcomers whip around, startled like deer in headlights.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a tuxedo trying to coax his pet iguana off the Muni steps at Civic Center, while the bus just sat there. Classic San Francisco chaos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think ‘La Rambla’ is the heart of Barcelona, but really, it’s the little plazas in Gràcia where life buzzes with locals and tapas flow.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by the way they struggle with 'cava' vs. 'champagne' at the local bar—it's always the foreigner asking for a bubbly 'on the rocks.'

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think ‘stopping off at the pub’ means a quick pint, not a three-hour social marathon with mates you just met.
Last weekend on the 59, an old bloke fell asleep and missed his stop. The driver had to wake him up three times before he got off at Crystal Palace. Classic Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: Last summer, I watched a kid sell lemonade outside a Kebab shop while a group of old men played chess right on the sidewalk. That felt like home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a kebab shop that doubles as an art gallery. The walls are plastered with local graffiti and the döner's still cheap!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they’re local when they can name the taqueria that’s still open at 2 AM on a Tuesday, no hesitation. The tourists never find it.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus today that stopped for a full five minutes because a guy was arguing with a pigeon. Classic Tenderloin moment.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just about Gaudí and tapas, but they miss the hidden murals and quirky boutiques that reflect the real local vibe.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by the way they still order a 'café con leche' instead of just a 'café'. It’s all in the little details.
